hypothesis: If equivariance-inducing training regularization (such as equigrad) causes non-equivariant architectures to converge toward the same representational manifold as architecturally equivariant models, then systematically interpolating the strength of this regularization during training will produce a monotonic increase in cross-architecture representational alignment that plateaus at a value indistinguishable from fully equivariant models, and this alignment plateau will be reached at a lower regularization strength for in-distribution data than for out-of-distribution data, providing a quantitative criterion for determining the minimum equivariance-inducing regularization needed to achieve foundation-level generality without the computational cost of full architectural equivariance.
